reviews.response:
Dear Ronnie,

Thank you for sharing your experience at Double Tree by Hilton Naha Shuri Castle.

We sincerely apologize for not meeting your expectations during your stay. We understand that the condition of the building and the location may not have been ideal for you. 

Additionally, we are sorry to hear about the long wait for breakfast. We are currently working on improving our service to ensure a more efficient experience for our guests. 

Your comments are valuable to us, and we will take them into consideration as we strive to enhance our offerings. 

We hope you will give us another chance in the future. Thank you again for your input.

reviews.title: The water facilities are old.
reviews.text: The room was spacious and comfortable. Also, the view from the room was wonderful. However, the water facilities were very old. Additionally, cleaning was not thorough, and it was disappointing to find dust around the air conditioning and small trash in the corners of the floor. I also think it would be difficult to access without a car. If you are more focused on sightseeing rather than enjoying your stay at the hotel, that's fine! The breakfast was also satisfactory!

reviews.title: What is hospitality?
reviews.text: The desk for Hilton tours is expected to make you wait. After about five minutes, I was approached. Instead of being taken inside the breakfast venue, I was directed to the lounge in front of the lobby. A group of elementary school students went inside before me. I wonder if it’s the elementary school students or adults who have the sensitivity to want to come back and share reviews. It should be easy to understand if you think about it. It's disappointing that with a waiting list of about 10 to 20 people, they can't make that judgment. Also, even though the seats in the lounge in front of the lobby were empty, they seated us tightly next to each other. With the surroundings being empty, how can anyone have a comfortable experience sitting so close together? Many people must be coming to stay because of the Hilton brand. How about reconsidering the overall quality of service?
reviews.response:
Yuki 様

reviews.title: Aged hotel not locating in downtown
reviews.text: First of all, we made a wrong reservation with this Double Tree, which is located closed to Shuri Castle.  There are two Double Tree hotels locate in Naha, one of them is locating in Asahibashi and this one is locating in Shuri Castle.  This hotel is too far from anywhere unless you want to visit Shuri Castle.  The hotel facility itself is aged especially the toilet.  It's unacceptable when we first seen the toilet with lots of scratch and the front desk staffs told us its a 45 years old hotel.  Beside of the toilet, the room renovated well and modern but interestingly the floor mat wasn't covered the entire floor in the room.  This hotel provides a paid shuttle bus starting from 5pm to Makishi but my question would be why its paid?  Don't you think your hotel is far away enough and not convenience?  Also, the bus schedule is not flexible at all!  Its started at 5pm so that means we would have to take the cab by our own before 5pm?!

reviews.title: Bad location but nice hotel
reviews.text: The hotel belong to JAL group is situated close to Shuri castle,which make it quite off the way, ,you have to walk up a pretty steep slope to the main road and walk for another 15-20 mins to the nearest monorail station or bus station.so we take taxi to everywhere.the hotel is typical Nikko hotel the room size are larger than most other japan hotel,and they serve a great morning breakfast ,I pay only US$80 for my room including b/fast
reviews.positives: Great rate andGood room size ,great breakfast ,quiet

reviews.title: Family stay with two older children
reviews.text: Good experience in hotel stay with family.On one hand ,the room size is not so big,but still with 4 standard size beds .The space inside the room is still ok.But the facilities inside toilet and bathroom is quite old.The space for wet clothes hanging  is not enough.On the other hand, dinner buffet is not  value for money.It is very nice to provide places for firework playing including lighting source provided.
reviews.positives: Nice staff,nice swimming  pool and good buffet breakfast selection

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: This hotel is a great location  IF your goal is to focus on Shuri Castle and all the very historical sights, which are awesomely close…however it is not convenient if you are needing a convenient store.  If you wanna get away from the hustle and bustle this may be for you… it’s a older location… and the room we were in showed it… The bed was decent… The bathroom was not… sink had a crack in it and the lever for the toilet had a layer of green crust on it…the tv had very limited options… what bothered me the most was the AC… initially we kept cranking up the AC to cool ourselves down because of the summer heat… it turns out regardless of how much we played with it, it seemed to have no effect on the air coming out…. Ultimately we had no control over the temperature control in our room…. If you have a high body temperature this may not be the hotel for you… we tried to make the best of the room, but the saving grace of this hotel is the outdoor pool!!  It’s old… however the amenities of a warm shower and the number of options to rinse off was great… since it’s the summer it was mostly families with children… and it’s open until 8 PM…. I wish I knew about the poolside barbecue available afterwards… you need to make reservations I think before 4pm to be able to participate… if you do the pool I highly recommend you try to do the barbecue, esp. at night… it’s very peaceful and beautiful… I was a little bit disappointed that the front desk didn’t bring these things to our attention beforehand… of course the warm cookie was great… there was one other family in front of us but it took us over 20 minutes to get our tickets even though they had about 4-5 staff members behind the long counter… it looks like you were being ignored however it’s a little tricky… they all wear the same uniform but each person plays a different role… so even though there’s a number of people technically there was only one person doing check-ins… it’s an old building with a lot of potential…

reviews.title: Mr. Lo
reviews.text: Ok but not great. breakfast is normal and need to pay for car parking and hourly extension fee
reviews.positives: near to City, shuttle service and have swimming pool

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: I used the tatami room since I had a baby. It was nice to be able to lay down a futon and sleep, and it was good to have the dining area separated so I could put the baby to sleep and have a late-night snack. However, there was a quite strong odor, either from the tatami or the carpet. Some parts of the hotel are somewhat aging, which can't be helped. There's a small convenience store, but it's not open 24 hours, and I don't think we need to use breakfast more than twice since there aren't many options. The swimming pool will not be operational from November, so we didn't get to use it. Still, it wasn't particularly far to go to various places. The hotel parking lot was large and nice. The soundproofing was excellent and the water pressure was good.

reviews.title: 良いホテルですが

reviews.title: old japanese styly hotel
reviews.text: This hotel seemingly built more than 25 years before and therefore it follows quite old fashioned Japanese hotel. Room size is unexpectedly small and sound proof is not so bad however, not good. Breakfast dishes are appreciated even for Vege and Moslim, quite accepted to many people. Restaurant is clean and room is narrow however well maintained. Close to Old capital area of Okinawa and good hill walk we can enjoy in the morning.
reviews.positives: Calm environment and good view

reviews.title: Better late than never.
reviews.text: I was a little disappointed that upon arrival they gave us a room that had an absolutely overpowering smell of mould: Obviously there had been a considerable flood of some kind in the room. The staff was, however, very cooperative and cheerful about moving us to another room, but one can't help but think that they are hoping to give the "smelly" room to someone who won't complain. Otherwise our stay was very nice: good complimentary breakfast and amenities, and the swimming pool is wonderful!
reviews.positives: The swimming pool. The view. The shower

reviews.negatives: It didn’t have any activities for teenagers on rainy days. Hilton Odawara has an amazing array of activities, but this was a bit disappointing

reviews.title: Good for the Cost
reviews.text:
The hotel is clean and the staff are warm. Although, the staff sounded inexperienced as they could not confidently answer if you ask them about tourist spots in the area. Google was our "person" to go! 
 Location-wise, not as good but using taxi eases this problem. Breakfast is good but not as varied as other Hilton hotels. When we were there, the cook who was making eggs did not smile at all and was just making gestures. Not a pleasant sight to look at! To sum it up, you get what you pay for!
reviews.response:
Dear Vieda,

Thank you for sharing your feedback regarding your recent stay with us. We are delighted to hear that you found our hotel clean and our team warm and welcoming. Your kind words mean a lot to us.
We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience caused by the lack of confidence in providing local recommendations. We understand how important it is to have helpful guidance during your travels, and we will ensure our team receives additional training to better assist our guests in exploring the area.
Regarding breakfast, we appreciate your comments and will review our offerings to provide more variety in line with Hilton standards. We are also sorry to hear about your experience at the egg station. We will address this with our culinary team to ensure every interaction reflects the friendly and positive atmosphere we strive for.
Thank you again for your honest feedback. It helps us improve and deliver the experience our guests deserve. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you

reviews.title: Great value!
reviews.positives: Great views and friendly staff made everything wonderful
reviews.negatives: Sits in the middle of a crater, and they do not do a good job of explaining how to get there on foot with very small children, we had to take a dangerous hike to get to the hotel one day because some told us to just follow google. I didn’t realize until it was too late how dangerous our path was. Lesson was learned though!!!!

reviews.title: It's an old but good hotel.
reviews.text: Access is not very convenient, but they arrange taxis quickly, so I didn't feel much inconvenience. The room is in an old building, but it seems to have been renovated, and it is very clean, beautiful, and spacious. It has a unit bath, but the bathtub is relatively large and the washbasin area is also big, making it easy to use. The staff were also very courteous.

reviews.title: Fair for the price
reviews.text: Stayed at the hotel for two nights. Great service, especially the front desk staffers. No problem getting breakfast even w/out reservation. Strongly recommend the hotel to a young family; Guarantee you’d have a great time at the outdoor pool. Enjoyed the poolside BBQ as well.
reviews.response:
Dear Yoko,

Thank you very much for staying with us at the Double Tree by Hilton Naha Shuri Castle, and thank you very much for taking your valuable time to place comments for us.

We are thrilled to hear that you enjoyed your stay with us and that our front desk staff made a positive impression. 

It's great to know that you found breakfast convenient and that your family had a fantastic time at the outdoor pool. 

We appreciate your recommendation and hope to welcome you back for another memorable stay soon!

reviews.title: good smell
reviews.text: Despite it being a long holiday, I was able to stay at an affordable price. Moreover, since my original room was fully booked, they upgraded me to a larger room. The welcome cookies were delicious, and the room's lighting was lovely. Although the facility's age was somewhat undeniable, there was a pleasant scent throughout the building that put me in a good mood. However, the only complaint I have is that I had to wait in line for over 10 minutes during check-in. I think it was because there were many international guests, but the process took a lot of time; even though there weren't many people in line, each group took quite a while. I believe they should increase the number of staff at the front desk.

reviews.title: nice bed to sleep
reviews.text:
pool with beer and food!
that's we call a vacation!

reviews.title: A clean hotel, very friendly and helpful staff.
reviews.text:
Stayed 2 nights with my family, the room is spacious, 3 beds, big enough for 3 to sleep comfortably. The air-conditioning wasn't working at all, felt stuffy in the room. Had called the front desk for help, but they couldn't do anything except to ask us to open the windows for fresh air. Luckily the outside temperature was cool, so that helped. We had to sleep with the windows opened throughout the night. 
The hotel location is away from amenities, the nearest convenient shops are 20 minutes walk via small paths, not recommended to walk alone in the night. We took a taxi from hotel to Naha international airport, it's 20 minutes journey with smooth traffic in the early morning, taxi fare was around ¥3650. Overall experience with this hotel is good as the staff are very friendly and helpful. However, if you are looking for easily available amenities and car rental services, this is not for you.

reviews.title: I like pools and cafes.
reviews.text:
Thank you for taking care of us during our 2-night, 3-day stay.

First, there was a forgetfulness regarding the cookies that were supposed to be provided as a service. I thought I might receive them later, but after spending a night without receiving them, I inquired and they promptly addressed the issue!

Next, I requested to borrow a stroller, but I was given a broken one. They also responded quickly when I brought this to their attention.

I understand that the staff is working hard despite being busy, but it seems there were quite a few instances of minor oversights...
However, it was great that they responded immediately.

Our room was very clean!
It was a bit disappointing that there was a gap in the double bed... I handled it by putting a towel in between.
There were complete amenities, water, and a bathrobe available.
I also received soap from Shuri Castle.
If requested, bed guards can also be borrowed!

While the room was clean, it was unfortunate that the toilet and bathroom somehow felt old relative to everything else.
And although we were in a suite, there were oddly two toilets... haha.

Having a pool on the first floor was nice too.
Additionally, the drinks and cakes at the café were delicious!

Thank you very much.

reviews.title: Edge of Naha
reviews.text: The hotel was fine but the room was on the small size, no coffee,milk or sugar in the room just tea. Personally I think it's too far from town. It's a taxi ride to get to anywhere else.
reviews.positives: Friendly and welcoming staff, great pool area. Nice 20th floor bar

reviews.title: Good view and near the city

The queueing and seating system at the breakfast can be improved to get people in more quickly and eating; could be long waits to get in even though there are seats and could be an issue for those needing to leave at a certain time.
reviews.response:
Dear Kennith,

Thank you for sharing your experience at Double Tree by Hilton Naha Shuri Castle.

We’re glad to hear that you found the room updated and the overall cleanliness satisfactory. We also note your comment about the washrooms and will pass it along to our maintenance team for review.

Regarding breakfast, we understand that the queue and seating system can be slow during busier times. 

Thank you again for your valuable feedback. We hope to welcome you back on your next visit.

The breakfast was a buffet and delicious!

reviews.title: Overall comfortable stay if you have car if not prepare to cab alot
reviews.text: Located down a slope within a walkable distance from shuri castle. A good stay if u intend to visit there. There's a really nice small restaurant that we found while walking back from the castle, called teppan bar ao to blue open till late , 12 am or something. These stalls are gems you will probably miss if you are just driving around without exploring since google only has 19 reviews. I suggest exploring. Other than that it's pretty isolated from shops. If you are driving then it is not much of a issue. So that's my main concern for people who are not driving you have to cab alot which is what my family did alot but it should not be an issue if you are prepared.  Other than that room was clean , buffet was good , staff was helpful , there was a laundry service using coins. Very chill vibes. But nothing exceptional in my opinion , just good overall but definitely no faults either orher than it being in a rather secluded area. But if you are peace lover who wants to enjoy your stay in a less busy area then this will be pro instead of a con.  I love okinawa

reviews.title: Very good hotel.

reviews.title: After the long wait at checkin the stay was good. Air conditioner didn't work well.
reviews.positives: Breakfast was good
reviews.negatives: Had to wait a long time for check in

reviews.title: Good choice of hotel
reviews.text:
I spent 4 nights there.
I must say that the hotel is pretty great! I stayed in a family room, so I had a really gigantic space for my Luggages and four beds. 
The wifi strength is pretty decent, except it may suddenly disappear (seldom
Case).
The hotel is located on a hill, and so the best mode of transport isn't the public transport, but taxis or cars or tour buses. The hotel is close to Shuri-Jo so walking there was good. However, it's not too close to uptown shopping districts and stuff, so I recommend taking s taxi if you want to get there.
The service there is really really great. Staffs are approachable, friendly, goes the extra mile for you, and are all in all, helpful.
The place is clean, I like the hotel amenities, and I overall, rate this hotel a 4/5, minus one for some of the location and the odd smell along the hallway( like something old).
reviews.positives: 1. This hotel is really clean, just like the rest of Japan is.  2. The staff service is great.  3. The hotel has Family Mart, so it's easy to post mails, buy food and drinks and all sorts of snacks.

reviews.title: Hotel Nikko Naha Grand Castle
reviews.positives: Good

reviews.title: Nice vacation
reviews.text: We stayed for 3 nights to get scuba diving license in Okinawa. The hotel was really close by Shuri Castle. It is so easy to access the center of Naha. It was so reasonable and the room was so confortable. It has a convenience store within the hotel, so you can get anything that you want anytime. It was the great vacation ever.
reviews.positives: Location, service, and facilities

reviews.title: location is quite far away from the city
reviews.positives: quiet, staff are friendly, nice pool

reviews.title: generally good except there isn't Wifi
reviews.positives: cleanness, location, sufficient parking area (but ckarged)

reviews.title: Fabulous views of the ocean
reviews.text: the room was very small yet adequate.  there was an internet hook-up, free but slow. the hotel had an electric pot to boil water for tea or coffee.  the bed was comfortable. i think it must be a good hotel for families.  there is a good swimming pool and the location is advantageous to visit the castle.
reviews.positives: The breakfast buffet was amazing. There were plenty of dishes containing local vegetables and herbs as well as familiar "American type" dishes such as scrambled eggs and pastries.  The views from the restaurant were wonderful. The hotel is located on a hill, so views of the ocean and Naha are plentiful.

reviews.title: Perfect for sightseeing in Naha City!
reviews.text:
Overall, the staff's response was good. The interior was relatively clean as it had been renovated.

Shurijo Castle can be reached in about 10 minutes on foot using a shortcut from the hotel.

You can park freely in available spots. The payment of 1300 yen is made at checkout, but it's also possible to pay at check-in.

I added breakfast, but it was the first time in my life that I had to wait in line to enter the breakfast venue (around 7 am). You cannot enter the venue freely; instead, the staff checks your room number one by one and guides you to your seat.

The manners of Chinese and Korean guests were extremely poor, which was very unpleasant. It was common for them to cut in line, ignore staff when entering, and sit at other people's tables without permission.

reviews.title: Good environment with friendly staffs.
reviews.text: My stay at the DoubleTree was excellent, and the staff is truly what made the experience. From the warm welcome cookie at check-in to the friendly and professional team members in the restaurant, everyone went out of their way to be helpful. Our room was very clean and comfortable. Overall, a great experience and I would definitely stay here again.

reviews.title: There are staff members who can converse in Korean and they are very friendly.
reviews.text: I thought I booked the DoubleTree in the city due to the flight cancellation, but it was the DoubleTree in Shuniseong. It's far from the city and the convenience store is also far, but the staff who could speak Korean was extremely friendly, which was great! I lost my doll in the taxi, and the staff outside told the taxi not to leave and helped me look for it! The place is a bit outdated and far from the city, but everything was good!

reviews.title: Great Value For Money
reviews.text: We had a great time overall! The staff were very helpful, the pool was beautiful, and the breakfast in particular was great value for money! One thing to note is that you definitely need a rental car or be prepared to take a lot of taxis to get anywhere. Apart from the location, it is a good option for accommodation in Naha!

reviews.title: Luxury hotel at an affordable price
reviews.text: The hotel is in the mountain side near Shuri Castle and by taxi from Yui monorail Kenchomae station  takes about 10-15 minutes depending on traffic conditions and about 1,500-2,000 yen.  The hotel is clean. Facilities are excellent. Bathroom amenities like toiletries are somewhat luxurious in fragrance and relaxing. Check-in was smooth and free warm fresh cookies are given upon arrival. Request for higher floors are possible. The higher the floor the greater the view. Rooms are well lit and TV is big enough. Room size is wider than most business hotels in Naha and its one of the few hotels that has a big and wide swimming pool on the ground unlike those in the city which are in the rooftop. Breakfast is reasonable at 2,700 per head since going out on a taxi going to the city to eat would also cost you more. The downside is that there was no umibudo on the menu while we stayed. Hotel has a chapel for weddings. However the area surrounding the pool lacks cleaning like moss/lichens growing under the chairs. The 3ft pool was off limits while we stayed. I don’t know why they restrict it especially for adults. Pool could have been better if they can extend it up to 10PM rather than 8AM. Overall I would give the hotel 9.5/10 rating for comfort, class, and cost performance. The hotel would have been better if they can offer food orders for room service and their menu options would change more often and not repeat the same say after another.

reviews.title: It's good to stay near Nahak Airport.
reviews.text: There is a bit of a smell in the restroom, but the room is larger and nicer than expected. Also, the front desk staff is very friendly.

reviews.title: I was able to fully enjoy it.
reviews.text: I stayed for 2 nights in July. The hotel had a pool that was available from 9 AM to 8 PM, and the atmosphere was great! The room was spacious, allowing for a comfortable stay.

reviews.title: High-performing hotel
reviews.text:
I used it for business. The good part is the view. I stayed on the 19th floor, so the scenery was really nice.

It wasn't facing the sea, but the sunrise from Shuri Castle was very beautiful.

From Kokusai Street, it costs around 1000 yen by taxi. If the traffic is light, it takes about 10 minutes, but it can take around 20 minutes when it’s crowded.

One downside is that they don't have a great selection of alcoholic beverages.

Since there's no convenience store nearby, I recommend buying drinks beforehand for those who want to drink in the room.

Overall, I thought it was a good hotel.

reviews.title: Good Location
reviews.text: Hotel is a short distnce from Kokusai street, for food and shopping.  It is also fairy close to the airport.

reviews.title: Nice Hotel
reviews.text: The location was good, and people dtaff were firendly.  Used the pool once and it was nice.  Did not use any other facilities

reviews.title: Due to the influence of the typhoon...
reviews.text: The airplane did not fly, and I stayed overnight on short notice. It is a bit of a distance from Kokusai Street, so it's not very convenient, but since it's within 1,000 yen by taxi, it didn't bother me. The building had a slightly old feel to it, but it was very clean, and the staff's service was very pleasant. I arrived at night, and due to a typhoon, I couldn't enjoy the pool, but considering that, I think the cost-performance ratio is quite good. The cookies I received as a welcome gift were delicious!

reviews.title: Good hotel with bad location
reviews.text: With the brand of Double Tree, the room and hotel facilities are within our expectation. Hotel staff are really nice to help us book for tour and help translating for us. However, the location is not convenient at all. Though they arrange shuttle bus to downtown, they work at evening only. If you are thinking to leave hotel early, you better grab taxi from hotel.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: I used this hotel to catch a flight in the 10 o'clock hour the next morning. The hotel is located on a hill a bit outside of central Naha, so it is quite quiet at night. The nearby sightseeing spot, Shuri Castle, is about a 10-minute walk, and I took a taxi to get to Kokusai Street. In my case, including the dispatch fee, it was about 1,000 yen, so if you are traveling with family, I recommend taking a taxi. Breakfast was served buffet style and included local dishes such as Okinawa soba, goya, and shikuwasa juice.
reviews.response:
Shinya 様

reviews.text:

The location is also not an issue if you plan to use a rental car or taxi for outings. Shuri Castle is close by as well.

If I had to point out an area for improvement, there was a slight odor that was concerning. Could it be due to the tatami mats and humidity? I think it would be better if some measures, such as forced ventilation on days without users, were taken.

reviews.title: Hotel with an elegant feel
reviews.positives: Beautiful resort type hotel. Great restaurants.
reviews.negatives: The location is a little isolated.

reviews.title: Easily accessible in a good way.
reviews.text:
I used to think that Hilton was not for ordinary people like myself, but there happened to be a cost-effective plan with breakfast included, so I stayed for the first time. 

One notable aspect is the excellent service from the front desk staff. Many of the foreign staff members speak Japanese fluently, and all of them have truly wonderful smiles and manners. 

Additionally, the accommodation floors always have a pleasant scent, probably from aromatherapy. 

If I had to mention a personal disappointment, it would be that the hotel is located at the bottom of a steep hill, making me hesitant to walk around the city at night. Although taxis are readily available if requested, it's difficult for those who want to walk. 

That night, I opted for the restaurant buffet, but there were hardly any customers, and the staff lacked energy, creating a lonely atmosphere. I'm not sure if the incredibly high prices for alcohol were the reason. (It might be about double that of a casual hotel?) 

Also, during early November when I stayed, it was outside the period for the poolside BBQ plan, but since the nights in Okinawa are warm, I would definitely appreciate an extension of that offer.

reviews.title: Excellent location and facilities. Service, cleanliness and staff are top-notch.
reviews.positives: Stayed here before and previous experience convinced to stay again.
reviews.negatives:
Two requests not met:
1) Was promised to replace an extra bed with a crib and it took repeated asking to have this completed.
2) Request for late check-out was declined

reviews.title: I was able to spend my time very comfortably♡
reviews.text:
This time, I traveled with one elementary school child and a toddler. I was drawn to the fact that the hotel had a pool, so I decided to stay here. 
First of all, the welcome cookies were absolutely delicious! They were large, soft cookies with walnuts, and they had my favorite flavor. The lobby and the hallways to the rooms also smelled great, and the children were delighted, saying, "It smells really good here!" 
During our 3-night, 4-day stay, it was sunny every day except the last one, so we were able to swim in the pool from the morning, and the kids seemed to have a lot of fun. I was also able to enjoy myself (laughs). To help guardians relax, there were large sunshade umbrellas and mats, which were very much appreciated. 
The pool was so comfortable that I wanted to swim every day, but since it was my first time in Okinawa in several years, I also wanted to do some sightseeing and go to the ocean, so I regretted that we only got to use the pool once. 
We were able to borrow towels by the poolside, and there were showers and changing rooms, making it very comfortable. All the staff were very polite and I am really grateful for that. 
At the very end, I accidentally left an important set of keys in the safe and flew back home in a panic, but now it seems like it will turn into a good memory (laughs). Thank you for contacting me about that. 
If I have the opportunity again, this is definitely a hotel I would like to use. I was able to create wonderful memories, thank you very much.

reviews.title: During our stay, typhoon hit Naha and stuff were very nice and accommodating us.  We didn’t experience the power outage
reviews.positives: Breakfast.
reviews.negatives: It was bit far from beach, store, and restaurant.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: The room was spacious and very clean! It might be a bit inconvenient if you don't have a car, but it was easy to access various places! The staff were also very friendly, and I felt quite comfortable!

reviews.title: I highly recommend this property for a family visit to Okinawa. Great value and amazing staff.
reviews.positives: The property was perfect for a family vacation. The pool is great and cabs are readily available to take you around the island. The food is also excellent. The staff go above and beyond to make guest comfortable!

reviews.title: Wonderful hotel
reviews.text: It was very beautiful and the staff's response was all good. The room was also cleaned nicely and was comfortable. There is a washing machine on the 3rd floor, but there are only six of them, and since I stayed during Obon, it was very crowded and difficult. I think it would be better to have a larger coin laundry.

reviews.title: Recommended
reviews.text: The staff in the hotel are very nice and the facilities wonderful. Recommend to those who self-driving in Okinawa, the hotel does provide car park. Highly recommend this hotel to those who wish to stay.

reviews.title: Pool was closed :(
reviews.text:
My family and I visited here at the end of March. The hotel was very nice, as expected for this hotel chain. Our room was big and clean, but there was evidence of a past flood or leaks, however I didn't smell any mold. The room had an air purifier, so that was nice. 

The hotel is located very close to a World Heritage site, Shurijo castle and about 20 minutes drive from the airport. It was a satisfactory experience.

My biggest disappointment was that the pool was closed :( I should have expected this because in Japan, there is a definite swimming season, and Okinawa's seems to be April - October. 

The family mart located in the lobby of the hotel was great and did not mark up prices, a nice bonus :) We only ate at Serena, but the food was delicious and they served many local specialities. The hotel offers a shuttle in the evening to Kokusaidoori, which is a 1.5 km strip of souvenir shops and restaurants. We took the shuttle (100 yen each way) and it was very convenient. 

The staff were very nice, polite and they helped with baggage when entering and leaving the hotel. Their service made it feel like a more luxury experience. 

We rented a car and it was easy to park at the hotel. However, be warned that there is a 900 yen/day charge for parking.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Super

reviews.title: Great Hotel
reviews.text: we chose this hotel because it had swimming pool. but when we went there, they said the pools are not available for use.  other than that, everything else was ok. the breakfast buffet was great. the room and amenities were good also. the staff was courteous and helpful. we were using rental car, and found out there is a parking fee for the car,  yen a day.
reviews.positives: Wonderful Breakfast Buffet, Convenience store in the same building

reviews.title: I feel relieved by your always polite response.
reviews.text: This time it was just an overnight stay, but the attentive service from check-in to check-out was as reassuring as always. I was recommended an upgrade on the day, but once I entered, I didn't feel much of a renewal, which was a downside. The bathroom and toilet seemed updated, but I wondered if the shampoo dispensers being fixed was the only change because the bathtub felt like the old one, which was a bit disappointing. It was my first stay in a year, and having a Seven-Eleven nearby was very convenient. It was handy when there was a FamilyMart before for forgetting to buy souvenirs. Taking a morning walk at Shuri Castle is part of my routine in Okinawa, so this hotel is a must.

reviews.title: It was very comfortable.
reviews.text:
Although it is located about a five-minute taxi ride from Kokusai Street, it had a pool and offered a resort feeling. However, since it is in the city, I thought the view wasn't very good as you can't see the sea. The staff was very polite, and the room was not as cramped as I expected, even with three people staying in it.

I had a pleasant time there.

reviews.title: Very good.
reviews.text:
Good points:
- Very clean and tidy
- There is a pool
- Rich in amenities
- Six washing machines and six dryers
- Cleaned beautifully every day during my five-night stay
- Taxis can be called even before sunrise
- The food is delicious

Not so good points:
- It's unavoidable, but the pool is lukewarm
- While Shuri Castle is easy to get to, other locations are quite far. The station in particular.

Nothing else to note!

reviews.title: This quality at such a low price!
reviews.text: We stayed in a standard twin room with four adults in our family. It included breakfast and access to the pool. There is a parking fee of 1,200 yen. The facilities and equipment are somewhat old, but they are well renovated and cleaned, making them very clean and nice. In addition to the regular beds, we added two extra beds, and the room was spacious enough for us to relax. However, I felt the bathroom was a bit small. The breakfast was rich in variety, focusing on Okinawan cuisine. The pool was large, and the shower room had shampoo and soap available. The changing room and lockers were also clean. We borrowed towels and floaties for free. It seems that on weekends, the pool is exclusive for guests. It is a very cost-effective hotel.

reviews.title: I'm glad!
reviews.text: I used the bus instead of renting a car, but it felt a bit far. I think if I had rented a car, I could have reached the hotel without much trouble. The service and the response from the hotel staff were very good, and I was able to have a pleasant stay. The cookies I received at check-in were very delicious. Also, the free bath towels at the pool and the ability to use umbrellas and beach chairs for free were really nice. The hotel has one of the largest pools in Naha City, so I leisurely spent time by the poolside. The hotel staff greeted me warmly every time I passed by, which felt very nice.

reviews.title: Anniversary trip
reviews.text: I used this for a wedding anniversary trip. When I informed them in advance that it was an anniversary trip, they decorated the room with cute towels, which was touching! It's a bit away from Kokusai Street, but the location is excellent, and it's about 1200 yen by taxi from Kokusai Street. It's convenient for moving around, and while the facilities are a bit old, they are clean, and the cookies provided during check-in were just as delicious as the rumors suggested! I couldn't use the pool and breakfast as planned, but I would definitely like to repeat this experience!

reviews.title: プールがあったから良かった
reviews.text:
1歳7か月の双子と4人で利用しました。
食事会場では子ども用の椅子がありました。
プールも身長80cmの子供たちでも足の着くプールもありとても良かったです。
子ども用のパジャマは持参しました。
子どもたちが遊ぶ場所や物はありませんので、観光メインで寝るだけの予定の方に適していると思います。

那覇空港からホテルまで混む時間だと1時間以上かかってしまいますので、移動の時間は注意が必要だと思います。
想像以上の渋滞です。
スタッフの方は丁寧な接客で淡々と仕事をされていました。

reviews.title: Wheelchair friendly room
reviews.text: One of the guests is wheelchair bound and the accessible room was perfect.

reviews.title: Highly recommended
reviews.text:
There were 4 of us and they gave us 4 individual beds! We were so happy we all got good night rest.
The buffet breakfast was very good and we enjoyed the many choices.  
The staff was very helpful and courteous.
Location-wise, it's a bit far from the beaches but luckily we got to rent a car. It is walking distance to the Shuri castle but it's a steep climb.  
The pool area was very nice; with very clean showers. 
The only negative thing we can write about is the dryer in the laundry room. It could not dry at all. I hope they fix that small issue.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: Beautiful hotel in good location.  Beautiful views of the city.   Very friendly staff.  Outstanding service.  Great breakfast buffet.

reviews.title: Had a compact room
reviews.text: Another area in Naha, a little bit outside the town area on top of the hill. And you need a car to travel around the area. Very good night view from the top of the hill. Especially the sky bar! Car park for a fee and WI-FI a fee too.

reviews.title: NICE
reviews.positives: window view, swim pool, service

reviews.title: Great Location with Spacious,  Clean Hotel room
reviews.text:
What I like about this hotel :
1)  Excellent Location with lot of ample parking lots 
2)  Clean and Spacious Room with comfortable beds 
3)  Beautiful outdoor swimming pool 
4) 7-Eleven convenience store  located at the main lobby 
5) Has washing machines and dryer at level 3.
reviews.response:
Dear Delvin,

Thank you for sharing your experience at Double Tree by Hilton Naha Shuri Castle.

We’re thrilled you enjoyed our location and ample parking, the clean and spacious room, and the beautiful outdoor pool. It’s also great to know the 7-Eleven in the lobby and the laundry facilities on level 3 added convenience to your stay.

We look forward to welcoming you back soon. If there’s anything we can do to make your next visit even better, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

reviews.title: I was satisfied.
reviews.text: The room and the water facilities were clean and comfortable. The view from the room was also nice. The top floor bar was excellent. It was convenient to have a convenience store in the building. The breakfast buffet was average, though. Since it has the Hilton name, I may have had too high expectations. Google Maps said it was a 25-minute walk to Shuri Castle, but since I could take narrow alleys, I was able to get there in about 10 minutes. It is very convenient for exploring that area.

reviews.title: Spacious room with a great view.
reviews.text: The space was larger than in typical Japanese hotels, which was great. We were a group of three couples, six people in total, and they arranged tables for us to eat together every breakfast. It was easy to reach key locations like Kokusai Street, American Village, and Engine-no-Yu by rental car, and there was plenty of space in the hotel's ground parking lot. Especially, Shuri Castle was very close, making it great for a walk. I highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Great
reviews.positives: I booked this place as our relaxation day and it met our expectation. A little bit far from the station but they will help you to get a cab
reviews.negatives: The adult side pool was closed so I didn’t enjoy.

reviews.title: The experience was good.
reviews.text: I plan to visit Shuri Castle early the next morning after checking in. The hotel is very close to Shuri Castle, about a ten-minute walk, but the way has a lot of slopes. The downside is that it is a bit far from the city center and the monorail station; it costs about 1000 yen for a taxi from Kokusai Street to the hotel, so if you have a lot of luggage, taking a taxi is still recommended. There is a convenience store on the first floor of the hotel, which is a bit small but very convenient for shopping. The room is quite spacious, the front desk staff is friendly, and at 10,000 yen per night, it's a good deal.

reviews.title: Great facility. Wish we could have stayed longer to really enjoy it.
reviews.positives: Free parking. Smells nice. Clean and well kept. Nice view. Lots of free parking.
reviews.negatives: I wish the pool was open until later. Closing at 6pm in the summer seems like a waste. Didn’t know what time it closed so we missed our chance to enjoy it.

reviews.title: It was very comfortable.
reviews.text: I stayed for two nights. It's within walking distance of Shuri Castle. I usually use taxis, so the location was not inconvenient. I spent a leisurely day by the poolside, and the staff's consideration and comfort were excellent. Overall, it feels a bit old, but in a way, it's a hotel with character. The twin room was very spacious, and I was able to spend my time comfortably. I will use it again.

reviews.positives: The staff was extremely kind and helpful. The location was within walking distance from Kokusai Street and Shuri Castle. It  was also not too far from the airport. Breakfast every morning was good. I enjoyed utilizing the pool as well.

reviews.title: The pool was nice.
reviews.text:
I had made a hotel reservation on a remote island, but all the boats to get there were canceled, so I made a reservation here on the day of arrival. I chose this place to enjoy the pool, which was spacious, and the kids were delighted. The changing rooms and showers at the pool were easy to use, and since drinks could be ordered at the bar, the adults were able to relax with some cocktails. The Hilton’s signature warm cookie hospitality and the detox water service were lovely, and after checking out the next day, they offered to store our luggage and provide towels and shower facilities when we went to the beach, which was a great help.

In terms of location, our family felt it was inconvenient because we rely on taxi transportation and it was quite a distance from Kokusai Street. The room had a luxurious feel, was calming, and was spacious, which was nice. However, the beds charged the same adult rate for my 8-year-old child, and there was only one type of hard bed available. The kettle was broken and unusable, but they brought a new one right away after I contacted them, so I was able to use it. There was also a small steamer available for skin care.

reviews.title: Very good hotel!
reviews.text:
The lobby of the hotel was particularly luxurious and clean. The staff's language and response were also good and satisfying! At check-in, I received a special cookie that was very delicious! The bed in the room was fluffy, and the amenities were always well-stocked; the pajamas were a two-piece set and felt great against the skin. 
One thing that caught my attention was that the bathroom walls and grout had noticeable black mold, and the area under the sink in the unit bathroom was not well cleaned, which was disappointing. 
Breakfast could be had without a reservation by just letting the staff know on the day, and there was a rich variety of options. The staff's service was good, and the meal was very pleasing. The buffet setting had plenty of natural light, creating a beautiful and pleasant atmosphere. It was great that the chef made fluffy omelets right in front of you with your chosen ingredients! 
There was also a private area, and the shallow pool made it feel safe for children. Towels were available for free, and staff were always present, so I felt comfortable. There were parasols and reclining chairs set up, allowing for a relaxing time, which was very nice! 
There was also a chapel, and when I visited, a wedding was taking place. In terms of sightseeing, Shuri Castle is about a 10-minute drive away, making it perfect for a morning stroll. The bus stop was nearby, and there was good access to areas like Sakaemachi and Kokusai Street.
reviews.response:
Tatsuya 様

reviews.text: I visited with my 18-month-old baby and wife. That day was my wife's birthday, so we received a room upgrade and free parking service. All the staff were very kind!

reviews.title: Great place with a great pool
reviews.text: Location is a little bit out of the way however the hotel was well kept and very accommodating

reviews.title: Recommendation for car rental customers.
reviews.text: The international street takes about 10 minutes if there's no traffic, and around 25 minutes on weekend evenings, but it wasn't very inconvenient to use a rental car for distance or location. The international street is a bit noisy, so it's recommended for travelers who want to stay in a quieter place while using a rental car. During my 2-night stay, I didn't see any Koreans; most guests were Japanese families and Westerners, and the parking fee was 1200 yen per day, which was paid at checkout. The pool isn't large, but it was nice to swim for a little while during the hot days after snorkeling for two days!

reviews.title: A comfortable hotel, and the kids really like the pool here.
reviews.text: The service at this hotel is excellent, with staff being very patient and attentive in explaining the facilities and introducing nearby restaurants. The children especially love the swimming pool here, particularly the smaller pool that is less crowded, where they can play for half a day. If you're driving, this is definitely a very good option.

reviews.title: It was convenient location
reviews.text: We liked the locations since its just 30mins away from the airport and american village and near kokusaidori. The room was quite spacious compared to other hotels in Japan. The breakfast was a buffet. We stayed 3days so 2days breakfast was all the same. I wish they changed some dishes but couldn’t complain more for the price we paid

reviews.title: Impressive experience
reviews.text: Since 2024, every year I visit Okinawa with my old mom (age 87),  she love this hotel very much and always stay in rm 1601,  I’m really impressed and thankful to the hotel that every time they arrange this room for us and keep my old mom happy, we will still come and hopefully for years!
reviews.response:
Dear Kam,

Thank you so much for sharing this wonderful feedback. We are truly honored that your mom loves staying with us in Okinawa, and that Room 1601 has become a special part of your annual visit. It is wonderful to hear that our team’s efforts to arrange this room have kept her happy.

We’ll continue to do our best to create a warm, comfortable experience for both of you. 

If there are any specific preferences or needs, we can prepare in advance, please feel free to let us know.

We look forward to welcoming you back for many years to come.

reviews.title: Okinawa is the best in February.
reviews.text: I stayed here for the first time. In February, it's not crowded, so you can relax. You can walk to Shuri Castle. The parking lot at Shuri Castle is very crowded, so it's helpful that you can walk from the hotel. The staff was also very friendly, and it felt good.

reviews.text:
Thank you for your hospitality from the end of 2025 into the new year. Initially, I was looking for hotels near major tourist spots like Kokusai Street and American Village, but by the end of my trip, I felt that there seemed to be a lot of traffic and a bustling atmosphere that wouldn't allow for relaxation. I'm really glad I chose this hotel where I could enjoy a more tranquil experience!

I wouldn't recommend this place for those expecting flashy performances during the New Year or wanting a hotel with a view of the beach, as they might think it’s not Okinawa without that! But I definitely want to recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful base for their Okinawa trip.

After all, it's a solid hotel and affordable!! (This is important.)

reviews.title: Family-friendly budget hotel for families with infants and young children.
reviews.text:
Advantages
Friendly staff.
Stroller rental service.
Welcome service with fruit-infused water and freshly baked cookies.
Excellent amenity service.
UCC coffee, decaffeinated Nescafé, various ramen drinks available in vending machines.
Spacious parking lot.
High-floor rooms with ocean views and stunning night scenery.
Variety of ready-to-eat meal options at the convenience store on the first floor.
Free self-service ice.
Coin laundry available for washing and drying.
No noise in the rooms.
Strong water pressure even on high floors.

Disadvantages
If you use the dryer thinking it’s like a Korean dryer, your laundry won’t dry even after an hour of drying.
Recommended to only dry wet swimsuits.

The night view of the pool was beautiful, and I could see the monorail passing by from the room, which made the view truly enchanting.
The pool staff lent us baby water play toys (duck squeaky toys), which was great fun, and the shower and restroom next to the pool on the first floor were close and provided towels, making it very convenient.

It was a 10-minute drive to Don Quijote Nago, and there were no parking difficulties after midnight.
It took 20-25 minutes to Oda Car Rental, and after returning the car, it was a 20-minute drive to the airport. We had breakfast and checked out at 8 AM for an 11 AM flight, and everything went smoothly without any inconvenience.
I booked one night on the last day of the trip, but due to time constraints, I couldn’t visit Shuri Castle or the international street, so next time I will need to book two nights to thoroughly explore everything.
Thanks to the friendly responses from the staff, I wrapped up my trip comfortably and enjoyably, creating great memories.
Thank you!!♡

There were military or police staying on the same floor, so it was a bit noisy, but the soundproofing wasn't bad. The bottom of the bathtub is very slippery, so be careful. The staff are very friendly; although their English could use some improvement, it didn't affect the service. I understand a little Japanese, so communication was smoother for me. However, the staff can understand English as well.

reviews.title: Atraso no check-in.
reviews.text: O Hotel é muito bom, porém no nosso dia, tivemos um probleminha pois não havia equipe de limpeza suficiente, isso resultou no atraso do nosso check-in, chegamos no hotel quase às 18h e os quartos não estavam prontos ainda, devido a falta de funcionários de limpeza, recebemos as chaves após às 18h, o funcionário da recepção, foi muito educado e nos deu o café da manhã  como cortesia, porém, achamos muito desagradável esse atraso acontecer logo no Hotel Hilton por ser uma rede muito conhecida e renomada, deveriam se preparar melhor para esses imprevistos e não afetar o cliente. O quarto era espaçoso, camas confortáveis, achei o banheiro um pouco antigo. Jantar e café da manhã estavam deliciosos. A piscina é grande e tem barzinho e lanchonete, BBQ, para comer sem precisar sair da piscina e ir pra outro lugar. Tem outros restaurantes dentro do hotel tbm, só tivemos esse inconveniente no atraso do check-in, espero que não aconteça mais.

reviews.title: 不便で融通が利かないのでこれがヒルトン？
reviews.text: 先日までハワイ島のヒルトンに泊まっていたのですが、これがヒルトンなのかというほど差が大きかったです。まずは、最寄り駅からヒルトンにいくにもかなり時間とタクシー代がかかります。またプールが冬季は営業していないと書いていたのですが3月が冬季ならまだしもプールは掃除をしている状況で、その音がうるさくて、２時にチェックインしましたがとにかくゆっくりできない。コンパクトルームといっていたのですが、コンパクトすぎて部屋全体がベッドでして、動きにくいし、値段が値段だけに仕方ないと思っていたら、バスの掃除がおわっていないのか髪の毛がおちていて、さすがにきたなすぎて、泊まることもせず急用ができたのでチェックアウトしますと他のホテルに予約をいれてうつりました。部屋に水もなく普通最近はリゾートホテルなら水の１本くらいはあるのに、おいてもなく、さらに、１９階では自動販売機もなく、売店は２時チェックイン時に休憩に入っていてしまっているし、サービスの悪さにびっくりして、さらに、タクシーを呼んでも今の時間帯はなかなか来なくてという始末でさっさとチェックアウトして、お金はもったいなかったですが、キャンセルもできないし、ここまでのタクシー代も自己負担で、バスもなくとんなでもないヒルトンでした。

reviews.title: Si trova sicuramente di meglio per vivere un’esperienza più confortevole
reviews.positives: La presenza della piscina.
reviews.negatives: Struttura datata, camere fruibili ma con odore di muffa che si sente fin dal corridoio a causa dell’impossibilità di poter asciugare/ appendere anche un semplice asciugamano dopo la doccia oppure i costumi grondanti. Le escursioni sono riservate unicamente ai clienti giapponesi, come ci è stato espressamente detto dalla reception,e quindi siamo stati costretti a rivolgerci a siti terzi sostenendo costi extra. Per un’escursione abbiamo chiesto di poter utilizzare gli asciugamani della struttura e ci è stato negato e solo dopo aver “insistito”, ed a fronte di una cauzione, abbiamo potuto farlo. Reception che non parla inglese con tutti i limiti del caso. Piscina con una intera parte non accessibile quindi tutti stipati nello spazio disponile con acqua alta 120 cm. Manutenzione ridotta al minimo indispensabile. Colazione a buffet ed in 4 giorni per ben due volte mancavano elementi basici come il pane. Moquette con polvere ben visibile all’ingresso della stanza e così è rimasta per l’intero soggiorno. Balsamo finito e mai ricaricato.Da una struttura di questa categoria ci aspettavamo decisamente di meglio.

reviews.title: ヒルトンとは思えないホテル
reviews.text:
年末年始の家族旅行で利用しました。チェックイン時間の30分前に少し早めだが入室したいとフロントの女性に伝えた所、冷たく断られ、ホテル内で待っていましたが、14時のチェックイン時間になっても、部屋の支度（エキストラベットの搬入）ができていないのであと40分待ってと信じられない返事が返ってきました。ドリンクのサービスチケットを渡され、カフェで待ちましたが、70分待つのは、腹が立ちました。クリスマスだったのでサンタがキャンディーをくれました。ホテル内でのクリスマスディナーを別途予約していたので食べましたが、値段にしては、料理の内容が乏しかったです。ローストビーフとミートパイは美味しいですが、ケーキ類は微妙でした。サービスでいただいたジンジャークッキーは可愛くて子供も喜んでいました。室内は、シャワーカーテンが水腐りのような匂いがあり、気になりました。バスタブもお湯を張って入る気にはなれない感じでした。１５階だったので、３階の販売機、洗濯機は遠いですが、お菓子、アイスの自販機は便利でした。乾燥機の料金が高いのが気になりました。フロントの階にあるヒルトンリゾートのくじなどを勧める女性がハワイ、ハワイとうっとうしく、感じが悪く嫌でした。お土産店のおばさんも愛想が悪く、子供連れで入店したら、あいさつも無く変な顔をされました。一番びっくりしたのが、全館禁煙のはずなのに、１５階のフロアはタバコの臭いが充満し、フロントに通報しましたが、原因が分からないと言ったきり放置でした。建物自体、古いし、ほんとにヒルトン系列なのか、値段に見合わず損した気分です。
廊下、３階のフロアは寒いです。暖房が弱いのか、風邪を引きました。

ヒルトンなのにこんなことがあっていいのかなと思いました。
フェリーの時間に間に合ったからいいものの、タクシーがすぐに来ていなかったらどうなっていたのかと思うと責任問題です。

reviews.title: 期待しすぎたかも
reviews.text:
良かった点
・場所はいいと思った
・スタッフ対応は良かった

悪かった点
・12階に泊まったが窓が汚くて景色は楽しめなかった。何の為の高層階なのか。

・アイロンの使い方がわからなくて、結局持ってきたシャツを着るのを断念した。説明書を置くか、説明書のリンク先などを明記するべき。使い方はお客様で調べてというのはホテルのサービスとしておかしい。

・夕食ビュッフェは値段の割にクオリティが低く（手が込んでない料理ばかりが目立った）、品数も少なかった。
他のホテルでは、アイスはブルーシールやハーゲンダッツなど、キチンと明記していたが、（確かヒルトン小田原はハーゲンダッツだったと思う）こちらでは明記されていなかった。
念の為アイスのブランドについて質問したが、質問されたスタッフはなぜ質問されたのかが分かっておらず、一応確認してもらったが、結局、ブランドのアイスではなかった為、食べるのを辞めた。

・到着早々、美味しそうなミネラルウォーターを配られてた。サービスがいいなと感じたが、結局はオーナーシップの営業だった。営業された方に対しては問題なかったが、ヒルトンホテルとして、あのような形で営業させてる事がそもそも気に入らなかった。

以上から、普通のホテルとしてで有れば問題ないのかもしれないが、ヒルトンホテルとして見た時に、正直ガッカリした。
多分暫くは利用しないと思います。
いい噂を聞くようになれば再度利用するかもしれません。

reviews.text:

ゆっくり滞在すると言うよりも寝るために使うくらいの気持ちが良いかも

気になった点は以下の通りです

〈利点〉
・ホテルのプールは那覇市内では広い方で子どもが十分楽しめる
・プールはタオルと浮き輪が無料レンタル
・国際通りまでタクシーで1000円程度

〈難点〉
・プールのロッカーは100円返還なしタイプ（レンタル無料も100円玉必要です）
・繁忙期の朝食はヒルトンオナーズの上位クラス以外結構並びます
・部屋の広さ、設備はビジネスホテル並
・最寄りバス停からホテルまでは急坂

reviews.title: 古いが良いホテル
reviews.text: 施設は少し古いですが、落ち着きのある良いホテルです。

reviews.title: 台風の際に利用しました
reviews.text:
先日の台風で自宅が停電断水したため
当日午前中にアゴダで空室を確認し、予約しました。午後、1歳と3歳の子供を連れホテルに着くと、チェックインは長蛇の列でチェックインの際に「お部屋の準備に1〜2時間待つ」とのことで、待たせてもらいました。結局3時間待っても声がかからず、またロビーはお部屋案内待ちのお客さんで溢れかえっていて、皆さん疲弊した様子でした。こちらから2〜3度進捗状況を確認し3時間後お部屋に案内されました。待ち時間が長くてしんどかったですが、停電断水した我が家にとって、お部屋はとても快適で安心して過ごせました。

reviews.title: 古さは否めない
reviews.text:
ツイン・デラックス・スイートに泊まりました。部屋は広く清潔で快適でしたが、難点は浴室、トイレです。
古い施設なので換気が弱く、私はそれほどでもなかったですが、妻がカビの匂いが気になったようです。
食事は、まあまあと言ったところで、このクラスのホテルもっと頑張っているところはある、というのが率直な感想です。
次にここに泊まりたいか、と問われると、他のホテルでいいかもと思いました。

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
夜バーからの眺めが夜景で綺麗でした。
朝ごはんがかなり、並んで待つので時間に余裕を持って行ったほうがいいです。

reviews.title: 少し古いホテル
reviews.text: 国際通りから割りと近いホテルでしたが、丘の上にあるようなところですので、お年寄りには向かないかもしれません。部屋も狭くあまり良くなかったように思えますが、一泊程度なら我慢出来る範囲のホテルです。
reviews.positives: スタッフの対応、立地、朝食

reviews.text: 夕食バイキングはサラダはレベルが高く大満足、ローストビーフも美味しい、蟹は長期冷凍品で期待はずれ、総じておすすめします。ウエルカムクッキーも熱々で頂きいい心遣いでした。staffも親切で楽しい滞在でした。部屋は価格にしては広くて不満なし。

reviews.title: 普通でした
reviews.text:
3泊４日の旅で、最終日が当ホテル。
普通のホテルでしたが、室内がカビ臭く、洗面所のタオルが黄ばんでいてビックリしました。ホテルの名前に負けないように清潔感アップしてほしいですね。スタッフの方は全員素晴らしい対応でした。感謝します。
reviews.response:
Taemi 様

このたびは、ご宿泊ならびに貴重なご意見をお寄せいただき、誠にありがとうございます。
せっかく数あるホテルの中から当ホテルをお選びいただいたにもかかわらず、お部屋の臭いや洗面所のタオルの状態につきまして、ご不快な思いをおかけし、心よりお詫び申し上げます。
本来、快適にお過ごしいただくべき空間でこのようなご指摘をいただく結果となり、誠に遺憾でございます。
今回いただいたご意見は、清掃・設備管理部門と速やかに共有し、換気・臭気対策の徹底、リネン類の品質管理の見直しを含め、早急に改善に取り組んでまいります。
ホテル名に恥じることのない「清潔感と快適さ」を改めて見つめ直す機会を頂戴したと受け止めております。
一方で、従業員の対応につきまして「素晴らしい」とのお言葉を頂戴し、大変光栄に存じます。
何よりの励みとなり、従業員一同心より感謝申し上げます。
今後は、ハード面・ソフト面の両面においてよりご満足いただけるホテルを目指し、
サービス向上に努めてまいります。
また機会がございましたら、ぜひ挽回の機会をお与えいただけましたら幸いです。
改めまして、このたびのご宿泊とご意見に心より御礼申し上げます。

プールのパラソル、風が強いと閉じてしまう。暑くていられない。
